_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 9-19R Dextrose (Revised 3/3/2025, MDHHS Reviewed 2025). Base Michigan template.
protocol_reference: Michigan Medication Protocol Section 9-19R: Dextrose — MDHHS Approved 3/3/2025, Reviewed 2025
contraindications: No medication-specific contraindications listed in the Michigan medication reference
precautions: Extravasation of hypertonic dextrose (D25, D50) causes tissue injury — confirm IV patency before administration
Dextrose should be pushed slowly, for example over 1-2 minutes
May utilize D10 for all ages at 5 mL/kg (0.5 g/kg) up to 250 mL
expected_effects: Rapid increase in blood glucose (effect within 1-3 minutes IV)
Improvement in mental status, seizure cessation, or behavioral improvement in hypoglycemia
side_effects: Tissue necrosis if hypertonic solution extravasates
notes: Dextrose dilution instructions from standard 50 mL D50W bag (500 mg/mL = 50%):
— D10 (10%): discard 40 mL from D50W bag, replace with 40 mL NS
— D12.5 (12.5%): discard 37.5 mL from D50W bag, replace with 37.5 mL NS
— D25 (25%): discard 25 mL from D50W bag, replace with 25 mL NS
May use D10 at 5 mL/kg for all ages as universal dose
Pediatric color-coded dosing table by weight and age (Grey/Pink/Red/Purple/Yellow/White/Blue/Orange/Green — reference MI-MEDIC)

dosing.notes: If using D50W: give 50 mL; if using D25: give 100 mL; if using D10: give 250 mL. Confirm IV patency before administering hypertonic solution
Universal dose applicable to all ages using D10 concentration; maximum dose per adult weight

dosing.dose: Per MI-MEDIC color-coded weight/age-based dosing table
dosing.notes: Color bands: Grey, Pink, Red, Purple, Yellow, White, Blue, Orange, Green — reference MI-MEDIC for exact doses by weight band
